sentence: Here is the SOAP note based on the transcript:
Subjective:
HPI: The patient is a  teenager who has been experiencing headaches for years, which initially started during her menstrual periods but have become more frequent and severe over time.
She describes the headaches as a pulsing sensation on the right side of her head, behind her eye.
She reports that exercising, particularly jogging, triggers the headaches.
She has also noticed ringing in her ears and blurred vision, which is intermittent.
The patient denies numbness or tingling in her hands, feet, or face.
Past medical history: NOT MENTIONED
Review of systems: The patient reports headaches, ringing in her ears, and blurred vision.
She denies any other symptoms.
Current medications: NOT MENTIONED
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure was reviewed and found to be normal.
Physical exam: The patient's heart exam is normal, with no murmurs, rubs, or gallops.
The funduscopic exam reveals papilledema, which is swelling behind the eye.
The patient's cranial nerves 1-12 are intact, and her cranial reflex is normal.
The patient's gait is normal on musculoskeletal exam.
Test Results: NOT MENTIONED
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient presents with symptoms concerning for idiopathic intracranial hypertension, which is a condition characterized by increased pressure in the brain without a known cause.
Problem list: Idiopathic intracranial hypertension; suspected
Plan:
I will order an MRI to confirm the diagnosis and rule out any other contributing conditions.
I will also perform a spinal tap to evaluate the pressure in the brain and potentially alleviate symptoms by removing fluid from the brain.
I will refer the patient to a neuro-ophthalmologist for a complete eye exam.
I will work with the patient's insurance company to obtain preapproval for the MRI.
Follow-up: The patient will be scheduled for an MRI and will be followed up with the results.
